specification.topic-statement: The LLM stream callback system processes provider streaming events and final response completion to accumulate, display, and store LLM output for an active agent.

scope.in-scope: Dispatching stream events (START, TEXT_DELTA, DONE, ERROR), accumulating response text and error messages, flushing the line buffer at completion, storing response metadata, and extracting tool calls and thinking blocks from the completed response.
scope.boundary: The provider sends stream events during delivery and a completion result at the end. This topic consumes both; it does not control the provider.
Text delta content is delegated to an external helper that manages line buffering and appends complete lines to the scrollback. This topic provides the raw text; the helper performs the display work.
Completed lines, flushed buffer content, and blank separator lines are appended to the scrollback by this topic. The scrollback itself is managed externally.

Metadata storage (model, finish reason, token counts), usage event rendering, and tool call extraction are performed by external helpers called from the completion phase. This topic calls them in sequence.

field.name: type
field.note: START | TEXT_DELTA | THINKING_DELTA | TOOL_CALL_START | TOOL_CALL_DELTA | TOOL_CALL_DONE | DONE | ERROR

behavior.stream-event-dispatch:
Each incoming stream event is dispatched by type. The agent context is mutable throughout.
If a previous assistant response string exists in the agent, it is freed. The streaming-first-line flag is set to true.
If the text field is non-null, the text is forwarded to the text delta handler for line buffering and display. Null text is silently ignored.
No action. Thinking content is not displayed during streaming.
TOOL_CALL_START, TOOL_CALL_DELTA, and TOOL_CALL_DONE events produce no action. The provider accumulates tool call data internally.
Token counts from the event's usage data are stored into the agent: input_tokens, output_tokens, and thinking_tokens.
If the error message field is non-null: any previously stored http_error_message on the agent is freed, and the new message is duplicated into agent-owned memory. If duplication fails due to out-of-memory, the process aborts.
The stream callback never returns an error to the provider. All events including ERROR events return a success result. Error information is stored on the agent for later display.
behavior.completion-processing:
Called once after the full response has been received (or failed).
Logging of success/failure status, model, and token counts is performed conditionally at compile time. When enabled, a JSONL record is produced and written to the debug logger.
The accumulated assistant response string is scanned for any character that is not a space, tab, newline, or carriage return. If any such character is found, had_response_content is set to true.
If a streaming line buffer exists in the agent (a partial line that has not yet been appended to scrollback): - If the streaming-first-line flag is set and an output prefix exists: the prefix (a filled circle symbol followed by a space) is prepended to the buffer content, and the combined string is appended to scrollback as a single line. Memory for the combined string is allocated; if allocation fails, the process aborts. - Otherwise: the buffer content is appended to scrollback directly.
The line buffer is then freed and set to null. The streaming-first-line flag is cleared.
If had_response_content is true, an empty line is appended to the scrollback as a visual separator before the usage display.
Any previously stored http_error_message is freed. If the completion indicates failure and an error message is present, the message is duplicated into agent-owned memory. If duplication fails, the process aborts.
Executed only when the completion succeeded and the response object is non-null.
An external helper is called to store the response model identifier, finish reason (converted from enum to string), and token counts (input, output, thinking) onto the agent.
An external helper computes total tokens (input + output + thinking). If total is greater than zero, a structured usage event is rendered to the scrollback. Otherwise, an empty line is appended.
An external helper iterates the response content blocks. Before iteration, pending thinking text, thinking signature, redacted data, and tool call on the agent are all cleared.
For each content block: - A thinking block stores its text and signature into the agent's pending thinking fields. - A redacted thinking block stores its data into the agent's pending redacted data field. - A tool call block creates a tool call record and stores it as the pending tool call; iteration stops after the first tool call.
Only the first tool call content block is processed. Subsequent tool call blocks in the same response are silently ignored.
The completion callback never returns an error regardless of outcome. All processing paths return success, with error information stored on the agent for subsequent display.
